steps.title: Identify the key chromium comparison
steps.content: The passage directly states that ferritic stainless steel contains “a higher percentage of chromium” than austenitic stainless steel. Cooking utensils use ferritic steel; washing machines use 200 series austenitic steel. This comparison is the decisive clue.

steps.title: Apply the comparison
steps.content:
C correctly states that washing machines will have lower chromium than cooking utensils.

The other choices contradict facts or lack support:
  • A wrongly attributes crystalline structure to ferritic
  • D ignores the iron requirement for magnetic properties
  • B creates an unsupported distinction between austenitic types
.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
